_note: H-4 (D3 axis-K): real, public-appropriate community facts surfaced on /community. Sourced from who/community/community_roles.md (the participation ladder) + the standard's governance model. Honesty line: only real practices — no fabricated member counts, no internal campaign jargon, planned != shipped. Ladder summaries are faithful one-line condensations of the real role docs.

ladder.name: User
ladder.summary: Clone the standard and use it for your own projects. No community interaction required — you get the full value locally.

ladder.name: Contributor
ladder.summary: Approve the improvements your agents surface and send them upstream through the public repository.

ladder.name: Quest Runner
ladder.summary: Run structured community experiments and submit results that turn standard questions into evidence, not opinion.

ladder.name: Steward
ladder.summary: Shape the standard's direction — design quests, review contributions, write migrations. Recognized by maintainers, never self-appointed.

governance.label: Chartered
governance.value: Operator-chartered — decisions are explicit and gated, never silent.

governance.label: Open standard
governance.value: MIT-licensed and versioned in public; the spec is the source of truth.

governance.label: Change process
governance.value: Public — improvements run through the repository as upstream contributions, reviewable by anyone.

governance.label: Attribution
governance.value: By convention, content files record who last edited them — humans and agents alike.

governance.label: Accountability
governance.value: Every unit of work closes with a written after-action report.

horizon: Member profiles, follows, activity feeds, and shared governance-voting surfaces are not built yet. They are being designed on the network's opt-in, local-first membership substrate — the same machinery the network page describes. Until they ship, the participation ladder and the open governance record on this page are the whole community layer. The network builds toward that horizon rather than implying it arrived.
